mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.5 percent signals immediate labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that sustains corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of co-occurring expansion periods, or interaction term loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, generating positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during post-shock recovery windows, with out-of-sample accuracy dropping below 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate drop below 1.0 percent triggers aggressive liquidity injections and risk-on positioning, producing immediate positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative or flat in 70 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS falls below 1.0 percent, breaking the liquidity transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rates indicates structural labor market tightening that compresses profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over three months.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields positive 60-day returns in more than 65 percent of cases, or the interaction coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a late-cycle expansion signal weakening consumer balance sheets and reduced construction activity, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns during late-cycle expansions, with directional accuracy falling below 48 percent over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in industrial production reflect strong manufacturing output and supply-side momentum that directly supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO uptrends produce negative 5-day forward returns in over 55 percent of observations, or the signal Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.
